prestazioni: Engine swap to a Ford 427 SOHC 'Cammer' (FE-series) with forged internals: Manley 4340 forged crankshaft (3.784" stroke), Eagle H-beam connecting rods (6.800" length), and JE forged pistons (12.5:1 compression ratio). Paired with a custom billet aluminum intake manifold by Trick Flow Specialties (TFS-51600001) and dual Holley 750 HP carburetors (0-80508S) for a target of 650+ HP on 110-octane race fuel.

Full drivetrain reinforcement: Tremec TKO-600 5-speed manual transmission (with a McLeod twin-disc clutch, RST-T1000) mated to a Ford 9" rear end with a Detroit Locker differential (28-spline, 3.73:1 gear ratio) and Moser Engineering 31-spline axles. The driveshaft is a custom 3.5" aluminum unit from Inland Empire Driveline with Spicer 1350 U-joints.

Suspension overhaul: Front coilover conversion using Ridetech HQ Series adjustable coilovers (with 250 lb/in springs) paired with a Global West tubular front subframe (GWF-100) and polygraphite bushings. Rear suspension features a Currie Enterprises 9" housing with Ridetech HQ Series coilovers (200 lb/in springs) and a Watts link system (Currie CE9WL) for lateral control. Braking is upgraded to Wilwood 6-piston front calipers (13.06" rotors) and 4-piston rear calipers (12.19" rotors) with DOT 4 racing brake fluid.
estetica: Full custom widebody conversion using a Rare Classics '67 Mustang Fastback bolt-on widebody kit (RC-67FB-WB), extending fenders by 3" in the front and 4" in the rear to accommodate 18x10.5" (front) and 18x12" (rear) BBS LM wheels with Nitto NT05R tires (275/35/18 front, 315/35/18 rear).

Aerodynamic package: Custom front splitter by Anderson Composites (carbon fiber, 3" overhang) paired with a Rare Classics ducktail spoiler (RC-67FB-SP) and a custom 3D-printed rear diffuser (integrated with the widebody kit). Side skirts are custom-molded to match the widebody flares, with functional brake cooling ducts (carbon fiber, 3" diameter) feeding the Wilwood calipers.

Interior overhaul: Full custom roll cage by Autopower Industries (8-point, mild steel, bolt-in) with a Sparco Pro 2000 seat (Evo II model) and Schroth 6-point harnesses. Dashboard is replaced with a custom carbon fiber unit from Vintage Air (integrated with Dakota Digital HDX gauges: 160-mph speedo, 10k-RPM tach, and oil pressure/fuel level senders). Steering wheel is a Nardi Classic 380mm (leather-wrapped) with a Flaming River tilt column.
